product.name: ArWoWear T11 Flame Pro
product.categorySourceGroup: Flame Retardant, Vat Dyed, Workwear Fabrics Resistant up to 100 Wash
product.titleDescriptor: 100% Cotton Twill, Flame Retardant Fabric, 360 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 360 g/m², 100% Cotton, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 3/1S Twill Fabric
descriptions: ArWoWear T11 Flame Pro is heavy weight 360 gr/m', 100% cotton, vat dyed, flame retardant workwear fabric.
It has flame retardant function with EN ISO 11612 and EN ISO 11611 certification.
It is durable up to 100 washing.
100% Cotton Twill, Flame Retardant Fabric, 360 gr/m'2
360 g/m², 100% Cotton, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 3/1S Twill Fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp>1600N; Weft>1000N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>30N; Weft>25N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612
functionIconLabels: >> ArWoWear T11 Flame Pro Data Sheet

acceptedMatches.rationale: Product explicitly states 'Welding Protection: Yes' with EN ISO 11611 certification, which is the standard for protective clothing used in welding and allied processes, and lists molten-metal splash protection directly.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: The fabric is explicitly rated and certified for electric-arc protection per EN 61482 with a specific ATPV rating and arc class, directly matching the energy sector's electric-arc protection use case.
EN ISO 11612 covers general flame/heat protection but is not the firefighter-specific EN 469 standard, and no explicit firefighting/turnout gear use is stated.
No explicit mention of military, police, or uniformed-service use in the product data.

evidence: Flame retardant, vat dyed, workwear fabrics resistant up to 100 wash
categorySourceGroup: Flame Retardant, Vat Dyed, Workwear Fabrics
primary.rationale: The product is explicitly framed as a general flame-retardant workwear fabric intended for coveralls, positioned within the broader FR-workwear category rather than a single narrow sector. While it carries welding and arc certifications, the primary commercial framing is general industrial protective workwear.

secondary.rationale: The fabric explicitly states 'Welding Protection: Yes' with EN ISO 11611 certification and molten-metal splash resistance, providing direct independent evidence of a welding/metalworking end use beyond generic FR properties.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: EN 61482 arc rating is present but no explicit statement of electrical utility or high-voltage work context beyond the certification itself, making it weaker than the explicit welding statement.
EN ISO 11612 is a general heat/flame protection standard, not the firefighter-specific EN 469, and no firefighting use is explicitly stated.
No explicit military, police or uniformed-service reference in the product data.

modelOutput.reviewReason: Product carries three overlapping protective certifications (welding, arc, general FR) without a single dominant explicit end-use statement, making the primary/secondary split between workwear-industrial, welding and energy somewhat ambiguous.
